note_text: My brother Samuel moved back to Portland last month. Everyone in the family just calls him Sammy.

expect.rationale: A 'calls him X' nickname is a name.nickname VALUE on the person, never its own entity.

note_text: My wife's full name is Celine Kitina Hopkins, but she goes by Celine. The kids call her Mom, and her old college friends still call her CeeCee.

expect.rationale: PRODUCTION BUG: a multi-nickname declaration is ONE person entity (max_entities 1); Mom/CeeCee are name VALUES, never entities.

note_text: I finally bought a 2019 Ford F-150. I've started calling it the Beast because it barely fits in the garage.

expect.rationale: 'aka the Beast' on a thing is a name.nickname VALUE on the vehicle plus an owns edge; the alias is never its own entity.

note_text: My daughter Priya was born on March 3rd, 2015 at the downtown hospital.

expect.rationale: birthDate value_json must be the bare date, not the sentence.

note_text: Weighed myself this morning: 178 lb. Doctor's chart says I'm 5 foot 11.

expect.rationale: Measurement value_json holds bare {value,unit}, never a sentence.

note_text: Reminder for the file: my blood type is O negative.

expect.rationale: Blood type is a timeless attribute; value_json is the bare 'O negative' datum.

note_text: Had dinner with Celine Hopkins last night. Celine Kitina Hopkins is finally feeling settled after the move.

expect.rationale: PRODUCTION BUG: two surface forms of one person in one note must resolve to ONE entity (max_entities 1), not a duplicate.

note_text: Took Mom to see Dr. Patel about the dizziness. She wants Mom to get an MRI next week and she ordered bloodwork too.

expect.rationale: Pronoun 'she' coreferences Dr. Patel; must not mint a 'she' entity.

note_text: Theo scored two goals at his soccer game this morning.
graph_context:
Owner/author: entity id 'owner-1' name 'Me' (Person).
Known entities:
- id 'ent-theo' name 'Theo Hopkins' (Person)
  fact Theo Hopkins.name.preferred -> Theo [attribute/asserted]
- id 'ent-celine' name 'Celine Hopkins' (Person)
  fact Me.spouse -> Celine Hopkins [relationship/asserted]

expect.rationale: Bare 'Theo' matching an existing entity resolves to ent-theo, not a new mint.

note_text: Got coffee with Chris yesterday, good catch-up.
graph_context:
Owner/author: entity id 'owner-1' name 'Me' (Person).
Known entities:
- id 'ent-chris-a' name 'Chris Alvarez' (Person)
  fact Me.worksWith -> Chris Alvarez [relationship/asserted]
- id 'ent-chris-b' name 'Chris Bennett' (Person)
  fact Me.sibling -> Chris Bennett [relationship/asserted]

expect.rationale: Two existing 'Chris' entities could match a bare 'Chris'; must be ambiguous, never guess.

note_text: We took the kids camping: my daughters Summer, Harmony, and Lydian had a blast.

expect.rationale: Enumerated relationship -> one edge per child (3 children edges, 3 entities), never one combined fact.

note_text: My nephews Marcus and Devon are twins — they turned six this week.

expect.rationale: Twins -> two person entities, never a 'twins' entity; the symmetric edge is the arbiter's to mirror.

note_text: Aunt Rosa is my mother's older sister; she's visiting from Lima next month.
graph_context:
Owner/author: entity id 'owner-1' name 'Me' (Person).
Known entities:
- id 'ent-mother' name 'Elena Vargas' (Person)
  fact Me.parent -> Elena Vargas [relationship/asserted]

expect.rationale: 'Aunt' implies female (inferred); direction of sibling edge is judgment.

note_text: We adopted a golden retriever this weekend. Her name is Biscuit and she's about two years old.

expect.rationale: Pet ownership -> ONE animal entity (max_entities 1); 'Biscuit' is its name value, not a 2nd entity.

note_text: Quick log on Dad: born April 2nd 1958, fasting glucose came back at 102 mg/dL today, he now lives in Tucson after the move, he saw his cardiologist last Tuesday, and he can't stand cilantro.

expect.rationale: Exercises five kinds; birthDate bare value is the hard part, predicate spellings advisory.

note_text: Big news: I start my new job at Northwind Labs on July 6th. Last day at my current place is June 30th.
graph_context:
Owner/author: entity id 'owner-1' name 'Me' (Person).
Known entities:
- id 'ent-acme' name 'Acme Corp' (Organization)
  fact Me.worksFor -> Acme Corp [relationship/asserted]

expect.rationale: A future employer is assertion=expected; must NOT supersede current worksFor today.
